title: Array indices should be placed between brackets
description: Array indices should be placed between bracketsAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?While C syntax considers array subscripts ([]) as symmetrical, meaning thata[i]andi[a]are equivalent, the convention is to put the index in the brackets rather than the array name. Inverting the index and array name serves no purpose, and is very confusing.Noncompliant code example10[P1] = 0; // Noncompliant dostuff(i[arr]); // NoncompliantCompliant solutionP1[10] = 0; dostuff(arr[i]);Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie P
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: dostuff(i[arr]);     // Noncompliant: variable index before array name
x = 5[data] + 1;     // Noncompliant: numeric literal before array
result = idx[buffer]; // Noncompliant: index variable before array
compliant: P1[10] = 0;          // Compliant: array name before index
dostuff(arr[i]);     // Compliant: array name before variable index
x = data[5] + 1;     // Compliant: array before numeric index
result = buffer[idx]; // Compliant: array before index variable

checks.message: Array indices should be placed between brackets. Use array[index] instead of index[array] for better readability and convention compliance.
